title: Kira and the Missing Acorns
synopsis: When all the acorns disappear from the park, young detective Kira teams up with her animal friends to solve the mystery and discover an unexpected culprit.

pages.text: Kira loved walking in Sunny Park every morning. She had curly brown hair and bright green eyes that sparkled when she smiled. Today, she wore her favorite yellow jacket with big pockets. Kira liked to collect pretty rocks and leaves on her walks. But today was different. Something was wrong in the park.

illustration.description: A cheerful girl with curly brown hair and bright green eyes wearing a sunny yellow jacket walks through a colorful park. Tall trees with golden and red autumn leaves surround a winding path. The morning sunlight filters through the branches creating a warm, magical atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Kira walking through Sunny Park on a beautiful morning

pages.text: "Where are all the acorns?" squeaked Nutkin the squirrel. He sat on a low branch, looking very worried. His fluffy gray tail twitched back and forth. "Every morning I find acorns under this big oak tree. But today there are none!" Kira looked around. The ground was empty. No acorns anywhere.

illustration.description: A worried gray squirrel with big expressive eyes and a fluffy tail sits on a tree branch talking to Kira below. The ground beneath a large oak tree is completely bare with no acorns visible. The squirrel's tail is twitched in concern while Kira looks up attentively.
illustration.alt: Nutkin the squirrel talking to Kira about the missing acorns

pages.text: "That is strange," said Kira. She put her hands on her hips like a detective. "Acorns don't just walk away by themselves." Nutkin nodded. "Exactly! Someone took them all. But who would take my breakfast?" Kira's eyes lit up. She loved solving puzzles. "Don't worry, Nutkin. We'll find your acorns!"

illustration.description: Kira stands with her hands on her hips in a confident detective pose, her curly hair bouncing as she looks determined. Nutkin the squirrel sits nearby looking hopeful. The scene shows them under the oak tree with scattered leaves but no acorns on the ground.
illustration.alt: Kira taking on the mystery like a detective while Nutkin looks hopeful

pages.text: Kira and Nutkin walked around the park looking for clues. "Look!" said Kira, pointing at the ground. "Tiny footprints!" The prints were smaller than Kira's hand. They led away from the oak tree toward the pond. "Those aren't squirrel prints," said Nutkin. "They're too small. And they have funny little marks."

illustration.description: Kira kneels on the ground examining tiny footprints while Nutkin peers over her shoulder. The prints are clearly visible in the soft dirt, leading in a trail toward a sparkling blue pond in the distance. Both characters look curious and focused on their detective work.
illustration.alt: Kira and Nutkin examining mysterious tiny footprints on the ground

pages.text: At the pond, they met Rosa the duck. She had beautiful white feathers and wore a small red bow. "Good morning, Rosa!" called Kira. "Have you seen anyone carrying acorns?" Rosa paddled closer. "Oh yes! Very early this morning. I saw someone with a big pile of acorns. They went that way," she pointed with her wing toward the berry bushes.

illustration.description: A elegant white duck with a cheerful red bow swims in a crystal blue pond, pointing with her wing toward colorful berry bushes. Kira and Nutkin stand at the pond's edge listening intently. The water reflects the morning sky and surrounding trees.
illustration.alt: Rosa the duck giving directions to Kira and Nutkin by the pond

pages.text: "Thank you, Rosa!" said Kira. They followed the footprints to the berry bushes. Behind the biggest bush, they found more clues. "Look at this!" whispered Nutkin. There were acorn shells everywhere. But the acorns were gone! "Someone had a big feast here," said Kira. "But who?"

illustration.description: Kira and Nutkin peek behind large berry bushes heavy with red and purple berries. The ground is scattered with empty acorn shells and bits. Both characters look surprised and intrigued by their discovery. Dappled sunlight filters through the leaves above.
illustration.alt: Kira and Nutkin discovering acorn shells behind the berry bushes

pages.text: Just then, Omar the rabbit hopped over. He had soft brown fur and long floppy ears. "What are you doing?" he asked. Kira told him about the missing acorns. "I can help!" said Omar. "My nose is very good at smelling. Let me try." He sniffed around the acorn shells. "I smell something sweet. Like honey!"

illustration.description: A friendly brown rabbit with long floppy ears and big round eyes hops toward Kira and Nutkin. Omar has his nose to the ground, sniffing carefully among the scattered acorn shells. His expression is helpful and determined as he uses his special smelling abilities.
illustration.alt: Omar the rabbit helping by sniffing for clues among the acorn shells

pages.text: "Honey?" said Nutkin. "But I don't eat honey with my acorns." Omar kept sniffing. The smell trail led deeper into the woods. "Follow me!" he said, hopping ahead. Kira and Nutkin hurried after him. The trees got thicker. The path got narrower. Where was Omar leading them?

illustration.description: Omar the rabbit hops ahead on a narrow forest path while Kira and Nutkin follow behind. The trees are dense with rich green leaves and golden sunlight streams through creating magical light beams. The path winds mysteriously deeper into the woods.
illustration.alt: Omar leading Kira and Nutkin deeper into the woods on the scent trail

pages.text: They came to a small clearing. In the middle was an old tree stump. "The honey smell is strongest here," said Omar, twitching his nose. Kira looked around carefully. "Wait," she said. "Do you hear that?" They all listened. There was a soft buzzing sound coming from inside the tree stump. "Bees!" whispered Nutkin.

illustration.description: A sunny forest clearing with an old, hollow tree stump in the center. Kira, Nutkin, and Omar stand nearby listening intently. Soft golden light fills the clearing and there are hints of movement around the stump. The characters look curious but slightly cautious.
illustration.alt: The three friends discovering the mysterious tree stump with buzzing sounds

pages.text: A tiny bee flew out of the stump. She had yellow and black stripes and looked very embarrassed. "Hello," she said in a small voice. "I'm Zara. I think I can explain about your acorns." Nutkin's eyes got big. "You took my acorns?" Zara nodded sadly. "I'm sorry. But I had a good reason!"

illustration.description: A small bee with bright yellow and black stripes and large apologetic eyes hovers near the tree stump. Kira, Nutkin, and Omar look surprised but not angry. Zara the bee has an embarrassed but sincere expression as she faces the group.
illustration.alt: Zara the bee apologetically explaining to the surprised friends

pages.text: "You see," said Zara, "my bee family is making something special. We're making acorn honey! It's for the big Forest Festival next week. All the animals get to share it." She looked at Nutkin hopefully. "I should have asked first. I'm really sorry." Nutkin's angry face turned into a smile. "Acorn honey? That sounds delicious!"

illustration.description: Zara the bee gestures excitedly with her tiny wings while explaining to the group. Inside the tree stump, other bees can be seen working on golden honey. Nutkin's expression changes from upset to intrigued and excited. The scene has a warm, golden glow from the honey-making activity.
illustration.alt: Zara explaining about the acorn honey while bees work in the background

pages.text: "Can we see?" asked Kira. Zara buzzed happily. "Of course!" Inside the stump, dozens of bees were busy. They had mixed ground acorns with flower honey. It smelled amazing! "Wow!" said Omar. "This will be the best festival treat ever!" Nutkin clapped his paws. "I forgive you, Zara. This is much better than plain acorns!" Everyone laughed. The mystery was solved, and they had all made a new friend.

illustration.description: All the friends peer into the hollow tree stump where busy bees are making golden acorn honey in hexagonal combs. The honey glows warmly and everyone's faces show wonder and delight. Zara beams proudly while other bees continue their work in the background.
illustration.alt: The friends marveling at the bees making acorn honey inside the tree stump

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was missing from under the oak tree?
questions.explanation: Nutkin the squirrel was upset because all the acorns were missing from under his favorite oak tree.

questions.question: Which animal helped by using their nose to smell for clues?
questions.explanation: Omar the rabbit used his excellent sense of smell to follow the honey scent trail that led to the tree stump.

questions.question: Who took the acorns?
questions.explanation: Zara the bee took the acorns to make special acorn honey for the Forest Festival.

questions.question: Why did Zara take the acorns?
choices: To make acorn honey for the Forest Festival
To hide them from other animals
questions.explanation: Zara and her bee family were making acorn honey as a special treat for all the animals at the upcoming Forest Festival.

questions.question: How did Nutkin feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Nutkin forgave Zara when he learned about the acorn honey and was excited about the Forest Festival treat.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instructions: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence from the story.

sentences.text: Kira loved walking in _____ Park every morning.
Nutkin the _____ was worried about his missing breakfast.
They found tiny _____ leading away from the oak tree.
Rosa the duck wore a small _____ bow.

pages.title: More Missing Words
pages.instructions: Complete these sentences with the right words.

sentences.text: Omar the rabbit could smell something _____ like honey.
The bees were making acorn _____ for the Forest Festival.
Zara the _____ felt embarrassed about taking the acorns.
